caseStudy.title: Personalised Email Commerce
caseStudy.description: Dynamic product recommendations driving email revenue.
caseStudy.overview: A home goods retailer sent the same promotional emails to its entire subscriber base, achieving industry-average engagement but leaving significant revenue on the table. The goal was to introduce product-level personalisation to increase email-attributed revenue.
caseStudy.problem: Email campaigns featured the same hero products for all subscribers regardless of browse and purchase history. Unsubscribe rates were rising, indicating that irrelevant content was eroding list quality. The email team lacked the tooling to personalise at scale.
caseStudy.solution: A recommendation engine was built on top of customer behaviour data, generating individualised product recommendations for each subscriber at send time. Dynamic content blocks in the email template were populated automatically, and send-time optimisation ensured each subscriber received emails when they were most likely to engage.

results.summary: Personalised email has become the highest-ROI marketing channel for the retailer. List health has improved as subscribers receive relevant content, protecting long-term deliverability.
